title: prod-api-asg cost spike — over-provisioned after a Terraform apply
alertText:
[CloudWatch Billing] TRIGGERED · SEV2 · prod-aws-account
Cost anomaly detected (anomaly-detector #BA-7781)
Run-rate: $1,180/day -> $9,800/day (8.3x) · EC2 driving 96% of the increase
prod-api-asg instance count: 8 -> 140 · avg CPU utilization 55% -> 6% (instances idle)
Request rate flat ~1,000 rps (no traffic increase to justify scale-out)
Correlated change: terraform apply @ 15:02 UTC (workspace: prod-api)
Ack: @finops-oncall · Opened 15:20 UTC
dashboardSpec: Cost board 'prod-api / spend': daily run-rate spikes $1.18k -> $9.8k at the terraform-apply marker; EC2 instance count jumps 8 -> 140; average CPU utilization collapses 55% -> 6% (the new instances are idle); request rate stays flat ~1,000 rps. High cost + low utilization + flat traffic = over-provisioning, not load.
logs:
15:02:11  terraform  INFO   Apply: aws_autoscaling_group.prod_api ~ desired_capacity: 8 -> 140, max_size: 12 -> 200
15:02:11  terraform  INFO   (plan authored by a find/replace that also bumped desired_capacity; intended change was an AMI id)
15:02:45  autoscaling INFO   prod-api-asg launching 132 instances to reach desired_capacity=140
15:06:20  autoscaling INFO   prod-api-asg now InService=140
15:10:00  cloudwatch  INFO   prod-api avg CPUUtilization = 6.1% (was ~55%); 134 instances effectively idle
15:12:30  alb         INFO   request rate = 1,002 rps (flat; no change vs last week)
15:18:40  billing     WARN   EC2 on-demand run-rate = $408/hr (was ~$49/hr); projected $9,800/day
15:19:10  billing     INFO   no Reserved Instance / Savings Plan expiry in window; increase is pure on-demand capacity
15:20:02  finops      INFO   prod-api-asg right-sized count for 1,000 rps at p95 SLO = ~8 instances
runbookMarkdown:
# prod-api — Cost Runbook

## 1. Cost spike from over-provisioned capacity
Symptoms: cost run-rate jumps, instance/replica count jumps, but CPU utilization DROPS and traffic is flat — you are paying for idle capacity. Find the change that raised capacity (autoscaling desired_capacity, HPA minReplicas, a Terraform apply) and revert it. Safe fix: set desired_capacity back to the right-sized value, e.g. `aws autoscaling update-auto-scaling-group --auto-scaling-group-name prod-api-asg --desired-capacity 8 --max-size 12`. Then add a budget/anomaly guardrail.

## 2. Traffic-driven autoscaling (DISTRACTOR — verify first)
Applies when request rate rose and CPU is high — legitimate scale-out. Here traffic is flat and CPU is 6%; this is not load.

## 3. Reserved Instance / Savings Plan expiry (DISTRACTOR — verify first)
Applies when a commitment lapses and on-demand replaces it at the same usage. Here usage (instance count) itself jumped 8 -> 140; no commitment expired.
groundTruthRootCause: A Terraform apply on the prod-api workspace changed prod-api-asg desired_capacity from 8 to 140 (an unintended edit — a find/replace meant to bump an AMI id also changed desired_capacity), over-provisioning the ASG. 140 instances now run at ~6% CPU serving a flat ~1,000 rps, so on-demand run-rate jumped ~8.3x ($1.18k -> $9.8k/day) for zero benefit. It's an IaC misconfiguration / over-provisioning, not traffic-driven scaling or a commitment expiry.
expectedSlackUpdate: :moneybag: SEV2 COST — prod-api-asg over-provisioned since 15:02 UTC. Run-rate $1.18k -> $9.8k/day (8.3x), 140 instances at 6% CPU on flat ~1k rps. Root cause: terraform apply bumped desired_capacity 8 -> 140 (unintended edit). Action: right-sizing desired_capacity back to 8 / max 12 + adding a budget-anomaly guardrail. No outage; this is pure waste. Next update 15:40. IC: @finops-oncall
